title: Emlen Physick House (Estate), First Floor Plan (HABS NJ-746, sheet 3)
era_or_year: house built 1879 (attributed to Frank Furness); HABS drawings 1973
creator: Historic American Buildings Survey; design architect attributed to Frank Furness; delineator H. J. McCauley
research_notes: Plan is sheet 3 (00003v.jpg) of HABS NJ-746 'Drawings' (resource index 1; resource 0 is photographs). Combined sheet: plan + fence elevation detail.
research_features: Complex Stick Style Victorian massing with multiple bay/projecting rooms, several fireplaces, and a porch. Combined sheet: the floor plan plus an inset elevation detail of the decorative property fence.
feature_highlights: restless Stick Style massing of many stepped rectangular volumes
multiple projecting bay rooms pushing past the main wall lines
numerous hatched masonry fireplaces set into the rooms
two separate straight-run stairs near the plan center
roofed porch shown by dashed roof/floor lines plus a stepped stoop
north arrow and graphic scale bar on the sheet
inset elevation detail of the decorative (now removed) fence and gate
supported_examples: straight walls at varied angles forming L-shaped and bayed rooms
custom non-rectangular room polygons for the projecting bays
door openings with swing arcs between many partitioned rooms
raster underlay calibration and the graphic scale bar
roadmap_examples: floor management (this is one of several stacked levels)
wall construction profiles/poché for masonry walls and chimneys
straight-run stairs as 2D symbols with floor-spanning topology

gap_features.why: a roofed porch (shown by dashed roof/floor lines) and a stepped stoop are covered-but-open spaces that must be drawable without full wall enclosure
the sheet carries a north arrow and a graphic scale bar as plan annotations
